companion_files.name: HANDOFF.md
companion_files.content:
# LLM_SESSION_HANDOFF.md

## Feature
- MBID release-correction and interactive release selection parity
- Plan: `.agents/plans/archived/mbid-release-correction-long-term-review/PLAN.md`

## Current State
- Feature extracted from Bundle F deferred scope (`Task 5.3`) on 2026-02-14.
- Backend + frontend feature implementation through Phase 4 is complete.
- Purpose is to isolate larger metadata UX + backend endpoint work from low-risk Bundle F polish.
- Phase 0 (`Task 0.1`, `Task 0.2`) is now complete in the active plan.
- Phase 1 completion:
  - `Task 1.1` complete (MusicBrainz lookup endpoints).
  - `Task 1.2` complete (interactive releases/grab endpoints).
  - `Task 1.3` complete (lookup + interactive route compat tests).
- Phase 2 completion:
  - `Task 2.1` complete (frontend API client wiring for lookup/release methods).
  - `Task 2.2` complete (metadata editor lookup UI + canonical MBID persistence path).
  - `Task 2.3` complete (release-selection modal integration on album/artist surfaces).
- Phase 3 completion:
  - `Task 3.1` complete (expanded regression coverage for metadata/release contracts).
  - `Task 3.2` complete (backend-direct + frontend-base probe evidence captured).
- Phase 4 completion:
  - `Task 4.1` complete (docs sync for CHANGELOG/README/fork delta docs).

## Scope Snapshot
- In scope for future slices:
  - MusicBrainz-assisted artist/release-group lookup endpoints.
  - Metadata editor lookup/selection integration.
  - Interactive release list + targeted grab flow.
- Out of scope until explicit promotion:
  - broad metadata refactor
  - unrelated Lidarr settings/schema changes

## Verification Evidence (Phase 3)
1. Regression suite pass:
   - `cd backend && npm test -- --runInBand src/routes/__tests__/enrichmentMetadataCompat.test.ts src/routes/__tests__/downloadsReleasesCompat.test.ts src/routes/__tests__/downloadsInteractiveCompat.test.ts src/routes/__tests__/enrichmentMusicBrainzLookupCompat.test.ts`
   - Result: pass (`4` suites, `26` tests).
2. Full-stack probe checkpoint:
   - Brought up local deps: `docker compose -f docker-compose.local.yml up -d postgres redis`
   - Ran probe backend: `http://127.0.0.1:3007`
   - Ran probe frontend: `http://127.0.0.1:3031`
   - Probed new endpoints on both URL surfaces:
     - `/api/enrichment/search/musicbrainz/artists`
     - `/api/enrichment/search/musicbrainz/release-groups`
     - `/api/downloads/releases/:albumMbid`
     - `/api/downloads/grab`
   - Result: all returned expected auth-gated `401 {"error":"Not authenticated"}`.
3. Probe teardown:
   - stopped local backend/frontend probe processes
   - `docker compose -f docker-compose.local.yml stop postgres redis`

## Operator-Facing Outcome
1. Metadata correction now supports guided MBID/RG MBID lookup in-app and persists canonical link corrections (not just temporary UI state).
2. Interactive indexer release selection/grab is available from album and artist surfaces.
3. URL guidance:
   - In split deployments, operators can configure the frontend base URL; local verification showed frontend-proxied behavior matches backend-direct path behavior for all new endpoints.

## Next Actions
1. If additional hardening is requested, next high-value step is authenticated full-path smoke coverage (seed user + token flow) for the new endpoints.

## Phase 0 Outcomes (2026-02-14)
- Adopt:
  - `GET /enrichment/search/musicbrainz/artists`
  - `GET /enrichment/search/musicbrainz/release-groups`
  - `GET /downloads/releases/:albumMbid`
  - `POST /downloads/grab`
  - `MusicBrainzLookup` and `ReleaseSelectionModal` component parity
- Adapt:
  - Preserve current auth/error/logger conventions.
  - Integrate through existing fork UI surfaces and centralized API client.
  - Preserve current non-destructive metadata override posture unless explicitly changed.
- Defer:
  - MBID-specific schema refactor and release-route consolidation beyond slice scope.

## Additional Outcomes (2026-02-14)
- MBID correction persistence is now implemented in metadata update routes:
  - artist `mbid` and album `rgMbid` corrections persist canonically.
  - album RG MBID corrections maintain `OwnedAlbum` mapping for library albums.

companion_files.name: PLAN.md
companion_files.content:
# MBID_RELEASE_CORRECTION_LONG_TERM_REVIEW_PLAN.md

## Document Status

- Last updated: 2026-02-14
- Feature: MBID release-correction and interactive release selection parity
- Parent context: `.agents/plans/archived/fjordnode-feature-port-bundle-f-metadata-library-polish/PLAN.md` (`Task 5.3` extraction)
- Scope: Planning/design only (no implementation in this document)
- Planning maturity: `Spec Outline` complete, `Refined Spec` complete, `Detailed Implementation Plan` complete
- Implementation state: Phases 0-4 complete (implementation + verification + docs sync)

## Why This Was Split Out

Bundle F intentionally deferred this work because it is a larger cross-surface feature (frontend metadata UX + backend search/download APIs) with broader regression risk than the completed metadata polish slice.

## Goal

Provide a safer, guided metadata-correction workflow where operators can:
1. find the correct MusicBrainz artist/release-group directly in the UI,
2. apply corrected MBIDs without manual copy/paste guesswork,
3. optionally run interactive release selection before grabbing downloads.

## Known-Gap Ledger Review

Reviewed on 2026-02-14:
1. Current workflow supports manual MBID editing but not assisted lookup/selection.
2. Fjord parity includes dedicated lookup/search endpoints and UI components for release selection.
3. Scope decision: treat as standalone long-term feature to avoid mixing with low-risk Bundle F polish.

## Full-Stack Request Path Baseline

1. Client URL/base path:
   - Frontend metadata editor and download UX paths under `/api/*` proxy usage.
2. Frontend proxy behavior:
   - `frontend/lib/api.ts` should remain the only API entry path from components.
3. Backend route path candidates:
   - `backend/src/routes/enrichment.ts`
   - `backend/src/routes/downloads.ts`
4. Downstream dependencies:
   - MusicBrainz API (artist + release-group searches)
   - Lidarr release search/grab flow
   - PostgreSQL (metadata persistence)
5. Deployment modes to validate:
   - `docker-compose.aio.yml`
   - `docker-compose.yml`
   - local host-run (`docker-compose.local.yml` + host frontend/backend)

## Spec Outline

1. Add backend endpoints for MusicBrainz lookup parity required by UI-assisted correction.
2. Add interactive release fetch/grab endpoints for manual operator control where needed.
3. Integrate lookup + release-selection components into metadata editing UX with clear fallbacks.
4. Preserve existing manual override flows and API contracts during rollout.
5. Add regression coverage and live full-stack probes before feature closeout.

## Refined Spec

### Constraints

1. No replacement of existing manual metadata edit controls until parity path is proven stable.
2. All new frontend calls must route through `frontend/lib/api.ts`.
3. Backend routes must preserve auth/error JSON conventions used elsewhere.
4. Any download behavior changes must include explicit guardrails for accidental wrong-release grabs.
5. Feature should be shipped in slices to control blast radius.

### Primary Touchpoints

1. Frontend:
   - `frontend/components/MetadataEditor.tsx`
   - `frontend/lib/api.ts`
   - (new/adapted) lookup/release components based on fjord patterns
2. Backend:
   - `backend/src/routes/enrichment.ts`
   - `backend/src/routes/downloads.ts`
   - `backend/src/services/musicbrainz.ts`
   - `backend/src/services/lidarr.ts`
3. Tests:
   - `backend/src/routes/__tests__/` (new compat suites)
   - frontend tests where coverage is practical for critical UX paths

### Acceptance Criteria

1. Operators can search/select artist and release-group MBIDs from UI without manual external copy/paste.
2. Interactive release list and targeted release grab flow works with clear success/failure signaling.
3. Existing metadata edit/reset behavior remains intact.
4. Backend-direct and frontend-base probes pass in local verification.

## Detailed Implementation Plan (Atomic Tasks)

### Status Legend

- `Pending`: Not started
- `In Progress`: Actively being worked
- `Complete`: Finished and includes completion summary

## Phase 0: Re-Context + Contract Freeze

### Task 0.1: Confirm fjord parity surface to port/adapt
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: Exact endpoint/component matrix with adopt/adapt/defer decisions.
- References:
  - `../lidify-fjordnode/frontend/components/ui/MusicBrainzLookup.tsx`
  - `../lidify-fjordnode/frontend/components/ui/ReleaseSelectionModal.tsx`
  - `../lidify-fjordnode/backend/src/routes/enrichment.ts`
  - `../lidify-fjordnode/backend/src/routes/downloads.ts`
- Completion summary:
  1. Adopt (additive parity surfaces):
     - `GET /enrichment/search/musicbrainz/artists`
     - `GET /enrichment/search/musicbrainz/release-groups`
     - `GET /downloads/releases/:albumMbid`
     - `POST /downloads/grab`
     - `frontend/components/ui/MusicBrainzLookup.tsx`
     - `frontend/components/ui/ReleaseSelectionModal.tsx`
  2. Adapt (fork-specific differences to preserve):
     - Keep auth/error conventions and logging style from current routes (`requireAuth`/`requireAuthOrToken`, `logger`, `{ error: string }`).
     - Keep API entry point centralized in `frontend/lib/api.ts`; add missing typed methods (`searchMusicBrainz*`, `getAlbumReleases`, `grabRelease`) and `AlbumRelease` type.
     - Integrate release-search UI against current fork surfaces (`frontend/features/album/components/AlbumActionBar.tsx`, `frontend/features/artist/components/AvailableAlbums.tsx`, `frontend/components/ui/PlayableCard.tsx`) rather than copying fjord wiring verbatim.
     - Preserve current metadata override model in early slices (non-destructive fields) until MBID persistence decision is explicitly promoted.
  3. Defer (out of current slice):
     - Broad metadata refactor/schema changes for MBID-specific override fields.
     - Consolidation of release-radar route (`backend/src/routes/releases.ts`) with interactive download route family.
  4. Re-context + direction checkpoint result:
     - Branch is clean (`feature/mbid-release-correction`), parity path remains correct, and Slice A backend lookup-only remains the lowest-risk start.
  5. Retrospective decision check:
     - Keep current phased direction; no rollback or course correction needed.

### Task 0.2: Define rollout guardrails and fallback behavior
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: Explicit fallback path to current manual MBID editing if lookup/release APIs fail.
- References:
  - `frontend/components/MetadataEditor.tsx`
  - `frontend/lib/api.ts`
- Completion summary:
  1. Metadata editor fallback contract:
     - Manual MBID text entry remains available regardless of lookup endpoint state.
     - Lookup failures return non-blocking feedback (toast/log), and operators can still continue with manual save/cancel flow.
  2. Interactive release fallback contract:
     - If `/downloads/releases/:albumMbid` or `/downloads/grab` fails or is unavailable, fallback remains existing auto-download path without blocking the page workflow.
  3. API compatibility guardrails:
     - New endpoints are additive only; existing metadata update/reset routes and payload contracts remain unchanged.
     - Existing route auth posture remains unchanged (no relaxing middleware requirements).
  4. Risk flagged for implementation phases:
     - Current enrichment metadata override routes do not persist `mbid`/`rgMbid`; Slice B/C must explicitly choose canonical update behavior vs schema-backed MBID overrides before exposing assisted writes as authoritative.
  5. Retrospective decision check:
     - Keep guardrails as defined; no hidden contract changes should be introduced in Slice A.

### Reuse/Consolidation Check (Phase 0)

1. Existing patterns reused:
   - Central API boundary in `frontend/lib/api.ts`.
   - Existing MusicBrainz and Lidarr service methods in `backend/src/services/musicbrainz.ts` and `backend/src/services/lidarr.ts`.
   - Existing route-compat test harness pattern in `backend/src/routes/__tests__/`.
2. Duplication intentionally kept:
   - Interactive release endpoints remain in `downloads.ts` for parity, while release-radar behavior remains isolated in `releases.ts`.
3. Consolidation deferred:
   - Cross-route release workflow unification and MBID-specific override schema consolidation deferred to a dedicated follow-up after feature slices ship safely.

## Phase 1: Backend API Foundation

### Task 1.1: Add MusicBrainz lookup endpoints for UI assistance
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: Auth-protected enrichment lookup endpoints for artist and release-group search.
- References:
  - `backend/src/routes/enrichment.ts`
  - `backend/src/services/musicbrainz.ts`
- Completion summary:
  1. Added `GET /enrichment/search/musicbrainz/artists` in `backend/src/routes/enrichment.ts`.
  2. Added `GET /enrichment/search/musicbrainz/release-groups` in `backend/src/routes/enrichment.ts`.
  3. Added `searchReleaseGroups(query, artistName?, limit)` helper in `backend/src/services/musicbrainz.ts` to keep release-group querying inside the existing MusicBrainz service boundary.
  4. Preserved existing auth posture (`router.use(requireAuth)`), additive route behavior, and `{ error: string }` response shape on failures.
  5. Retrospective decision check:
     - Keeping lookup query construction in `musicbrainzService` (instead of route-level raw fetch) is the correct path for cache/rate-limit reuse and lower duplication.

### Task 1.2: Add interactive release listing/grab endpoints
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: Download endpoints for release list and targeted grab workflow.
- References:
  - `backend/src/routes/downloads.ts`
  - `backend/src/services/lidarr.ts`
- Completion summary:
  1. Added `GET /downloads/releases/:albumMbid` in `backend/src/routes/downloads.ts` with:
     - Lidarr availability guard (`400`),
     - album lookup + artist-add retry flow,
     - normalized release payload mapping for UI (including size formatting and quality fields),
     - `404` when the album cannot be resolved in Lidarr.
  2. Added `POST /downloads/grab` in `backend/src/routes/downloads.ts` with:
     - required payload validation (`guid`, `lidarrAlbumId`),
     - download-job creation for interactive grabs,
     - explicit failed-job transition when Lidarr grab fails.
  3. Added helper formatting function for release payload size strings.
  4. Regression coverage:
     - new `backend/src/routes/__tests__/downloadsInteractiveCompat.test.ts`
     - existing `backend/src/routes/__tests__/downloadsReleasesCompat.test.ts` kept green.
  5. Verification evidence:
     - `cd backend && npm test -- --runInBand src/routes/__tests__/downloadsInteractiveCompat.test.ts src/routes/__tests__/downloadsReleasesCompat.test.ts src/routes/__tests__/enrichmentMusicBrainzLookupCompat.test.ts` (passed)
     - `cd backend && npm run build` (passed)
  6. Retrospective decision check:
     - Keeping interactive endpoints inside `downloads.ts` is the correct near-term path for parity and minimal blast radius; deeper release-route consolidation remains deferred.

### Task 1.3: Add backend route compatibility tests
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: Regression coverage for lookup route contracts, auth, and error mappings.
- References:
  - `backend/src/routes/__tests__/`
- Completion summary:
  1. Added `backend/src/routes/__tests__/enrichmentMusicBrainzLookupCompat.test.ts`.
  2. Covered success/failure contracts and validation behavior for both new lookup endpoints:
     - short-query validation (`400`)
     - success response field mapping
     - upstream failure mapping (`500` with error message)
  3. Verification evidence:
     - `cd backend && npm test -- --runInBand src/routes/__tests__/enrichmentMusicBrainzLookupCompat.test.ts src/routes/__tests__/enrichmentMetadataCompat.test.ts` (passed)
     - `cd backend && npm run build` (passed)
  4. Retrospective decision check:
     - Focused route-compat tests were sufficient for Slice A; broader integration probes remain deferred to Phase 3 full-stack checkpoint.

## Phase 2: Frontend Metadata UX Integration

### Task 2.1: Wire API client methods for new lookup/release endpoints
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: Typed API methods in centralized client.
- References:
  - `frontend/lib/api.ts`
- Completion summary:
  1. Added `AlbumRelease` API type in `frontend/lib/api.ts`.
  2. Added interactive release API methods:
     - `getAlbumReleases(albumMbid, artistName, albumTitle)`
     - `grabRelease(options)`
  3. Added MusicBrainz lookup API methods:
     - `searchMusicBrainzArtists(query)`
     - `searchMusicBrainzReleaseGroups(query, artistName?)`
  4. Retrospective decision check:
     - Centralizing these methods in the shared API client remains the correct approach for query/auth consistency and component-level simplicity.

### Task 2.2: Integrate MBID lookup assistance in metadata editor
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: Search/select workflow embedded in existing editor without breaking manual entry.
- References:
  - `frontend/components/MetadataEditor.tsx`
- Completion summary:
  1. Added `frontend/components/ui/MusicBrainzLookup.tsx` with:
     - artist/release-group search,
     - external MusicBrainz link-outs,
     - manual MBID paste fallback.
  2. Replaced plain MBID inputs for artist/album in `frontend/components/MetadataEditor.tsx` with `MusicBrainzLookup` while preserving direct track MBID input.
  3. Updated album editor callsite (`frontend/features/album/components/AlbumHero.tsx`) to pass `rgMbid` seed value and `artistName` filter context.
  4. Resolved persistence contract needed for assisted correction:
     - `backend/src/routes/enrichment.ts` now accepts canonical `mbid`/`rgMbid` updates in metadata routes.
     - album RG MBID corrections now update `OwnedAlbum` mapping for library albums to preserve ownership semantics.
     - added regression assertions in `backend/src/routes/__tests__/enrichmentMetadataCompat.test.ts`.
  5. Retrospective decision check:
     - Enabling canonical MBID persistence in the same slice was required to avoid a misleading UI that appears to “fix” MBIDs without actually persisting corrections.

### Task 2.3: Integrate release-selection modal flow
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: Operator-triggered release selection + grab path with clear confirmation/errors.
- References:
  - `frontend/components/MetadataEditor.tsx`
  - candidate new UI files under `frontend/components/ui/`
- Completion summary:
  1. Added `frontend/components/ui/ReleaseSelectionModal.tsx` for interactive indexer release discovery and targeted grab.
  2. Integrated modal launch from album page action bar (`frontend/features/album/components/AlbumActionBar.tsx`) via new `Search` control next to quick download.
  3. Added artist-page entry path:
     - extended `frontend/components/ui/PlayableCard.tsx` with optional `onSearch` action,
     - threaded search action through `frontend/features/artist/components/AvailableAlbums.tsx`,
     - wired modal state/render in `frontend/app/artist/[id]/page.tsx`.
  4. Verification evidence:
     - backend compat suites and build pass:
       - `cd backend && npm test -- --runInBand src/routes/__tests__/enrichmentMetadataCompat.test.ts src/routes/__tests__/enrichmentMusicBrainzLookupCompat.test.ts src/routes/__tests__/downloadsInteractiveCompat.test.ts src/routes/__tests__/downloadsReleasesCompat.test.ts`
       - `cd backend && npm run build`
     - frontend lint run executed and currently fails due pre-existing repository lint debt unrelated to this feature; no new fatal lint rule introduced by this slice.
  5. Retrospective decision check:
     - Dual-entry interactive release selection (album + artist surfaces) is the correct UX compromise for operator control without disrupting existing quick-download behavior.

## Phase 3: Verification and Safety Nets

### Task 3.1: Expand regression coverage for metadata/release flow
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: Tests that prove no regression in existing metadata update/reset contracts.
- References:
  - `backend/src/routes/__tests__/enrichmentMetadataCompat.test.ts`
  - `backend/src/routes/__tests__/downloadsReleasesCompat.test.ts`
- Completion summary:
  1. Added metadata conflict-contract regressions in `backend/src/routes/__tests__/enrichmentMetadataCompat.test.ts`:
     - artist MBID uniqueness collision returns `409`.
     - album release-group MBID uniqueness collision returns `409`.
  2. Added reset-contract regression in `backend/src/routes/__tests__/enrichmentMetadataCompat.test.ts`:
     - album reset clears override fields and preserves canonical-link behavior.
  3. Added release-surface regression in `backend/src/routes/__tests__/downloadsReleasesCompat.test.ts`:
     - authenticated release-radar trigger still returns expected `501` not-implemented contract.
  4. Verification evidence:
     - `cd backend && npm test -- --runInBand src/routes/__tests__/enrichmentMetadataCompat.test.ts src/routes/__tests__/downloadsReleasesCompat.test.ts src/routes/__tests__/downloadsInteractiveCompat.test.ts src/routes/__tests__/enrichmentMusicBrainzLookupCompat.test.ts` (pass; 26 tests).
  5. Plain-English impact:
     - Operators can trust that MBID correction and release controls keep existing error/reset behavior instead of silently changing prior contracts.

### Task 3.2: Full-stack verification checkpoint
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: Recorded backend-direct and frontend-base probe evidence for new paths.
- References:
  - `docker-compose.local.yml`
  - host-run backend/frontend startup commands
- Completion summary:
  1. Started local verification dependencies with `docker compose -f docker-compose.local.yml up -d postgres redis`.
  2. Ran backend on `http://127.0.0.1:3007` and frontend on `http://127.0.0.1:3031` for probe-only verification.
  3. Probed all new endpoints through both backend-direct and frontend-proxied URLs:
     - `/api/enrichment/search/musicbrainz/artists`
     - `/api/enrichment/search/musicbrainz/release-groups`
     - `/api/downloads/releases/:albumMbid`
     - `/api/downloads/grab`
  4. All probes returned the expected auth-gated `401 {"error":"Not authenticated"}` response on both URL surfaces, confirming path wiring and proxy parity.
  5. Operator URL statement:
     - split-stack users can configure the frontend base URL for these paths; backend-direct and frontend-base behavior matched in local host-run verification.
  6. Teardown completed:
     - stopped backend/frontend probe processes and `docker-compose.local.yml` postgres/redis dependencies.

## Phase 4: Docs + Closeout

### Task 4.1: Sync user-facing docs for shipped behavior
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: Scoped updates only if feature is shipped.
- References:
  - `CHANGELOG.md`
  - `README.md`
  - `docs/FORK_DIFF.md`
  - `docs/FORK_DIFF_COMMITS.md`
- Completion summary:
  1. Updated `CHANGELOG.md` (`1.5.0` unreleased) to record MBID lookup-assisted correction and interactive release selection/grab behavior.
  2. Updated `README.md` feature bullets to reflect guided metadata correction/release search capability.
  3. Updated `docs/FORK_DIFF.md` grouped delta summary with MBID correction + interactive release-selection parity notes.
  4. Updated `docs/FORK_DIFF_COMMITS.md` appendix metadata/entries for this feature’s committed history.
  5. Plain-English impact:
     - Operators now have documentation that matches what the UI/API actually does for fixing bad metadata links and selecting indexer releases.

## Historical Slice Breakdown (Completed)

1. Slice A: backend lookup contract
- Delivered in Phase 1 (`Task 1.1`, `Task 1.3`).

2. Slice B: metadata editor assisted search
- Delivered in Phase 2 (`Task 2.1`, `Task 2.2`).

3. Slice C: interactive release selection/grab
- Delivered in Phase 2 (`Task 2.3`).

4. Slice D: verification/docs hardening
- Delivered in Phase 3 (`Task 3.1`, `Task 3.2`) and Phase 4 (`Task 4.1`).

## Next Follow-up Slices

1. Slice E: authenticated full-path verification
- Active plan: `plans/current/mbid-release-correction-authenticated-verification/MBID_RELEASE_CORRECTION_AUTHENTICATED_VERIFICATION_PLAN.md`

2. Slice F: MBID validation and conflict UX hardening
- Planned/staged for future implementation.

3. Slice G: interactive release modal hardening
- Planned/staged for future implementation.

4. Slice H: route/service consolidation for release flows
- Planned/staged for future implementation.

companion_files.name: PROMPT_HISTORY.md
companion_files.content:
# PROMPT_HISTORY.md

## 2026-02-14 Session Prompt History (Feature Extraction Setup)
1. User requested splitting Bundle F's two larger deferred items into standalone future features.
2. Session created this MBID-focused long-term feature track from Bundle F deferred `Task 5.3`.
3. Added planning artifacts:
   - `MBID_RELEASE_CORRECTION_LONG_TERM_REVIEW_PLAN.md`
   - `LLM_SESSION_HANDOFF.md`
   - `PROMPT_HISTORY.md`
4. No implementation started; this track currently holds planning and slice recommendations only.

## 2026-02-14 Session Prompt History (Phase 0 Contract Freeze)
1. User requested starting work on `mbid-release-correction-long-term-review` and to follow `AGENTS.md`.
2. Session re-contexted against the feature handoff/plan and ran a feature-local stale-link verification for plan references.
3. Completed Phase 0 tasks in plan docs:
   - Task 0.1 parity matrix (`adopt`/`adapt`/`defer`) against `../lidify-fjordnode` routes/components.
   - Task 0.2 rollout guardrails and fallback behavior for lookup/release failures.
4. Captured critical implementation risk:
   - current metadata override routes do not persist `mbid`/`rgMbid`, so assisted lookup write semantics must be explicitly decided before Slice B/C.
5. No product code implementation started in this session.

## 2026-02-14 Session Prompt History (Slice A Backend Lookup Implementation)
1. User approved implementation start ("Let's begin").
2. Implemented backend lookup contract in `backend/src/routes/enrichment.ts`:
   - `GET /enrichment/search/musicbrainz/artists`
   - `GET /enrichment/search/musicbrainz/release-groups`
3. Added service helper in `backend/src/services/musicbrainz.ts`:
   - `searchReleaseGroups(query, artistName?, limit)`
4. Added regression coverage in `backend/src/routes/__tests__/enrichmentMusicBrainzLookupCompat.test.ts`.
5. Verification run:
   - targeted route compat tests passed
   - backend TypeScript build passed
6. `Task 1.1` and `Task 1.3` marked complete; `Task 1.2` left pending by slice scope.

## 2026-02-14 Session Prompt History (Task 1.2 Interactive Endpoints)
1. User confirmed proceeding after metadata persistence clarification.
2. Implemented interactive release endpoints in `backend/src/routes/downloads.ts`:
   - `GET /downloads/releases/:albumMbid`
   - `POST /downloads/grab`
3. Added interactive endpoint compat coverage in `backend/src/routes/__tests__/downloadsInteractiveCompat.test.ts`.
4. Re-ran impacted compat suites and backend build:
   - downloads interactive compat: pass
   - existing downloads/releases compat: pass
   - enrichment lookup compat: pass
   - backend TypeScript build: pass
5. Phase 1 is now complete; next step requires phase-boundary confirmation before entering Phase 2 frontend integration.

## 2026-02-14 Session Prompt History (Phase 2 Frontend Integration + MBID Persistence)
1. User requested checkpoint commit first, then continue.
2. Created checkpoint commit:
   - `39b58ba feat: add MBID lookup and interactive release download endpoints`
3. Implemented frontend Phase 2 work:
   - API client methods/types for MusicBrainz lookup and interactive release flow.
   - `MusicBrainzLookup` component + metadata editor integration.
   - `ReleaseSelectionModal` component + album/artist surface wiring.
4. Resolved previously flagged persistence gap by updating backend metadata routes:
   - artist `mbid` and album `rgMbid` corrections now persist canonically.
   - album MBID corrections update `OwnedAlbum` mapping for library albums.
5. Expanded regression coverage and reran impacted backend tests/build:
   - enrichment metadata compat: pass
   - enrichment lookup compat: pass
   - downloads interactive compat: pass
   - downloads/releases compat: pass
   - backend TypeScript build: pass
6. Ran `cd frontend && npm run lint`:
   - fails due extensive pre-existing lint debt in unrelated files (React Compiler + hooks + settings sections), plus one non-blocking warning in new modal dependency list.
7. Phase 2 tasks are complete in plan artifacts; next boundary is Phase 3 verification.

## 2026-02-14 Session Prompt History (Phase 3 Verification + Phase 4 Docs Sync)
1. Continued after checkpoint commits to complete verification and closeout.
2. Expanded regression coverage:
   - `backend/src/routes/__tests__/enrichmentMetadataCompat.test.ts`
     - artist MBID conflict `409`
     - album RG MBID conflict `409`
     - album reset override contract coverage
   - `backend/src/routes/__tests__/downloadsReleasesCompat.test.ts`
     - authenticated release-radar trigger retains `501` contract
3. Verification run:
   - `cd backend && npm test -- --runInBand src/routes/__tests__/enrichmentMetadataCompat.test.ts src/routes/__tests__/downloadsReleasesCompat.test.ts src/routes/__tests__/downloadsInteractiveCompat.test.ts src/routes/__tests__/enrichmentMusicBrainzLookupCompat.test.ts`
   - result: pass (`4` suites, `26` tests).
4. Executed full-stack checkpoint using local host-run ports:
   - backend `http://127.0.0.1:3007`
   - frontend `http://127.0.0.1:3031`
   - backend-direct + frontend-base probes for:
     - `/api/enrichment/search/musicbrainz/artists`
     - `/api/enrichment/search/musicbrainz/release-groups`
     - `/api/downloads/releases/:albumMbid`
     - `/api/downloads/grab`
   - all probes returned expected auth-gated `401 {"error":"Not authenticated"}`.
5. Local probe services were torn down after evidence capture.
6. Updated plan and handoff artifacts to mark Phases 3 and 4 complete.
7. Completed docs sync updates for `CHANGELOG.md`, `README.md`, `docs/FORK_DIFF.md`, and `docs/FORK_DIFF_COMMITS.md` for this feature scope.
8. Ran additional verification:
   - `cd backend && npm run build` (pass)
   - `cd frontend && npm run lint` (fails on existing repo-wide lint debt; feature warning in `ReleaseSelectionModal` was resolved)
9. Ran feature-local stale-link verification for `plans/archived/mbid-release-correction-long-term-review/`; all referenced `plans/current|archived` paths now resolve.

plan.title: MBID_RELEASE_CORRECTION_LONG_TERM_REVIEW_PLAN.md
plan.objective:
1. Add backend endpoints for MusicBrainz lookup parity required by UI-assisted correction.
2. Add interactive release fetch/grab endpoints for manual operator control where needed.
3. Integrate lookup + release-selection components into metadata editing UX with clear fallbacks.
4. Preserve existing manual override flows and API contracts during rollout.
5. Add regression coverage and live full-stack probes before feature closeout.
